manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: port, teacher, band, swing, crash, phoenix, plate, berry, pirate.
Your opponent's team words are: contract, capital, face, green, telescope, oil, root, kiwi.
Distractor words are: heart, plastic, war, pan, ninja, millionaire, snowman, orange.
The kill words are: .

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: port, teacher, band, swing, crash, phoenix, plate, berry, pirate.
Your opponent's team words are: contract, capital, face, green, telescope, oil, root, kiwi.
Distractor words are: heart, plastic, war, pan, ninja, millionaire, snowman, orange.
The kill words are: .

CLUE: HOME
TARGETS: port, plate, teacher

The word heart was guessed but is an innocent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: port, teacher, band, swing, crash, phoenix, plate, berry, pirate.
Remaining words for your opponent are: contract, capital, face, green, telescope, oil, root, kiwi.
Remaining distractor words are: plastic, war, pan, ninja, millionaire, snowman, orange.
Remaining kill words are: .

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: port, teacher, band, swing, crash, phoenix, plate, berry, pirate.
Your opponent's team words are: contract, capital, face, green, telescope, oil, root, kiwi.
Distractor words are: heart, plastic, war, pan, ninja, millionaire, snowman, orange.
The kill words are: .

Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. reasoning_details.text:
Thinking Process:

1.  **Analyze the Request:**
    *   Goal: Provide a single-word clue related to some of my remaining team words.
    *   Format:
        CLUE: <WORD>
        TARGETS: <TARGETS>
    *   Constraints: No extra text, short answers, clue must be semantically related, cannot be one of the words or contain parts of them.
    *   Context: This is a Codenames-style game.
    *   Current State:
        *   My Team Words (Remaining): teacher, band, swing, crash, phoenix, plate, berry.
        *   Opponent Words (Remaining): contract, capital, green, telescope, oil, root, kiwi.
        *   Distractor Words (Remaining): plastic, war, pan, ninja, millionaire, snowman, orange.
        *   Kill Words: None listed (empty).
        *   Previous Guesses: "heart" (distractor - turn ended), "port" (correct), "pirate" (correct).

2.  **Evaluate Remaining Team Words:**
    *   teacher
    *   band
    *   swing
    *   crash
    *   phoenix
    *   plate
    *   berry

3.  **Identify Potential Associations:**
    *   *teacher*: school, class, education, professor, staff.
    *   *band*: music, group, ring, strap, rubber, elastic, gang.
    *   *swing*: park, music (swing jazz), move, playground, seat.
    *   *crash*: accident, noise, computer, cymbal, stock market.
    *   *phoenix*: bird, fire, rise, rebirth, city.
    *   *plate*: dish, food, metal, license, home (baseball).
    *   *berry*: fruit, strawberry, blueberry, bush, red.

4.  **Find Connections between Multiple Words:**
    *   *band* + *swing*: Music genres (Swing band).
    *   *band* + *crash*: Cymbal crash? Band crash? Not strong.
    *   *plate* + *berry*: Food? Plate of berries.
    *   *phoenix* + *crash*: Plane crash? Phoenix crash? Not strong.

Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

    *   Constraint 2: No other text.

    *   Constraint 4: Clue must be semantically related to the target words.
    *   Context: This is a Codenames-like game. I need to help my partner guess my team's words while avoiding opponent words, distractors, and kill words.
    *   Current State:
        *   My Remaining Words: teacher, crash, phoenix, plate, berry.

If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: port, teacher, band, swing, crash, phoenix, plate, berry, pirate.
Your opponent's team words are: contract, capital, face, green, telescope, oil, root, kiwi.
Distractor words are: heart, plastic, war, pan, ninja, millionaire, snowman, orange.
The kill words are: .

TARGETS: port, plate, teacher

The word heart was guessed but is an innocent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Remaining kill words are: .

TARGETS: port, pirate

The words port, pirate were guessed correctly. Your teammate's turn ended there.

Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.
